thesis: Pure-play 'green metals' champion following strategic divestiture of steelmaking coal business to Glencore for $7.3B. Now focused on copper and zinc with best-in-class growth pipeline. Pending 'merger of equals' with Anglo American (expected mid-2026) will create Anglo Teck — top-five global copper producer with >70% copper exposure and $800M annual synergies. QB2 copper project in Chile is flagship growth asset. $5B net cash position provides fortress balance sheet.

drivers: QB2 achieves 1.6B lbs peak production by 2026-2027
Anglo American merger closes on schedule with $800M synergies
Anglo Teck becomes top-5 global copper producer with >70% copper exposure

risks: Copper falls to $3.50/lb on global recession
QB2 TMF (Tailings Management Facility) issues persist
Anglo American merger faces delays or fails
China demand slows more than expected

base_case.assumptions: Copper averages $4.25/lb; QB2 reaches 240kt by 2027; Anglo merger closes with normal execution; Synergies achieved as guided; Moderate copper market tightness.

catalysts.event: Anglo Merger Regulatory Approvals — Key milestone
Anglo Teck Merger Close — Transformational event

key_risks: QB2 TMF (Tailings Management Facility) sand drainage challenges delaying ramp-up
Anglo American merger execution and regulatory approval risk
Copper price volatility — high correlation to China demand
Chile political risk — royalty increases, regulatory changes
Merger integration complexity with Anglo American
